title: QA Failure Density Enumeration FAIL — documents the wrong-ratio failure mode that the FDE_RATIO gate would catch
_fixture_purpose: Documented FAIL fixture, well-formed under the schema. Demonstrates the failure mode that FDE_RATIO is designed to catch: a fixture declaring the wrong cosmos ratio (e.g., 8/81 instead of 72/81) — equivalent to the cert [194] CSM_AGENCY mismatch case, but expressed in this cert's vocabulary. The validator early-returns on result=FAIL (matching cert [194] convention csm_fail_wrong_agency.json) and only requires the fixture to be schema-conformant + carry a populated fail_ledger. The body fields below intentionally encode the wrong claim so a reviewer can see exactly what the FDE_RATIO gate would have flagged. This fixture is what a reviewer constructs to confirm a known failure mode is detectable by the validator without having to falsify the live FAIL_LEDGER.
source_attribution: (Kochenderfer, 2026) Algorithms for Validation §7.1; cert [194] qa_cognition_space_morphospace_cert_v1 (Sole, 2026; arxiv:2601.12837 via Dale, 2026). Mirrors the csm_fail_wrong_agency.json convention from cert [194].

exact_enumeration.method: DELIBERATELY WRONG: cosmos numerator is set to 8 instead of 72 to demonstrate FDE_RATIO mismatch detection.

fail_ledger.message: Deliberately wrong: cosmos ratio declared as 8/81 but exact enumeration on S_9 yields 72/81. If result were not FAIL, the validator would emit: 'FDE_RATIO: declared cosmos ratio=8/81, expected 72/81' and 'FDE_RATIO: declared cosmos count=8, computed 72'.
